title: Store Opening
description: Morning process to prepare the store for business: from shift handover to the first customer.

steps.title: Shift handover with security
steps.content: Arrive 30 minutes before opening. With security check: overnight incident log (break-ins, alarm triggers), safe seal integrity, lock condition. Sign the handover log. If anything is off — don't enter alone, call the manager first.
steps.key_takeaway: Arrival + security signature = your shift starts. Before the signature, it hasn't.

steps.title: Sales floor walkthrough
steps.content: Quick check: leaks (ceiling, fridges), broken displays, pest signs, floor condition. Lighting on? HVAC running? Registers booting? If you find a problem — photo + manager chat immediately, fix in parallel.
steps.key_takeaway: Walkthrough = 5 minutes. Breakages before opening aren't incidents. Breakages with customers around are.

steps.title: Display and price tag check
steps.content: Priority zones: checkout, hot spots, endcaps. Verify: every item has a price tag, shelf price = register price (spot-scan 10 items), no empty shelves on promo. Missing price tag = regulatory fine on inspection.
steps.key_takeaway: 10 random price scans. Mismatch = claim before opening.

steps.title: Shelf replenishment
steps.content: Deploy team for restocking: fast-movers first (bread, milk, water), then promos, then the rest. Rotation rule — FIFO (first in, first out): new stock behind, old stock in front. Check expiry dates in the checkout zone — first thing customers see.
steps.key_takeaway: FIFO. Old in front, new in back. Reverse order = write-offs = losses.

steps.title: Register setup
steps.content: Register: insert cash drawer, open shift in POS (previous day's Z-report must exist), check receipt paper, load change float (standard ~$30). Card terminal — test $1 payment and refund. Internet working?
steps.key_takeaway: $1 terminal test is mandatory. Finding out from a customer = failure.

steps.title: Team briefing
steps.content: 5 minutes before opening: quick huddle. Lay out the day plan (promos, new arrivals, delivery schedule), task each person (register, floor, replenishment). Reminders on promos: discounts, upsells. Ask for questions. Inspire, don't attack.
steps.key_takeaway: Huddle = 5 minutes. Everyone knows their task before the doors open.

steps.title: Opening the doors and the first hour
steps.content: Unlock doors exactly on time. The first hour is the most critical: peak load, cashier mistakes, first complaints. Don't retreat to the office — stay on the floor. Help with bagging, manage queues, suggest products. First impression = a week of loyalty.
steps.key_takeaway: First hour — on the floor, not in the office. Where the problems are, not where the reports are.

quiz.question: How early does the manager arrive?
quiz.explanation: 30 minutes is standard for handover, walkthrough, briefing, and register startup.

quiz.question: What is FIFO in shelf stocking?
options: First in, first out (old in front)
quiz.explanation: FIFO prevents expiry write-offs — the main job of shelf stocking.

quiz.question: Mandatory card terminal test?
options: Not needed if it worked yesterday
quiz.explanation: Only a real transaction confirms the full chain works.

quiz.question: What to do if an item has no price tag?
options: Remove from shelf or print a tag urgently
quiz.explanation: Selling without a price tag is prohibited — regulatory fine risk.

quiz.question: Where should the manager be during the first hour?
options: On the sales floor, helping the team
quiz.explanation: Peak load requires the manager to be where the customers are.

manager_checklist: Signed the shift handover log with security
Held team briefing 5 minutes before opening
Spent the first hour on the sales floor

kpi.target: 100% of days open exactly on time
kpi.how_to_measure: CCTV logs or register timestamp of first transaction
CCTV queue counter or cashier timer
Write-off records / revenue for the period
